title: Behind bars by choice: Chanchalguda Jail now lets citizens ‘feel the jail’ at ₹2,000 for one day
content: The 24-hour ‘Feel the Jail’ experience has been priced at ₹2,000, while those opting for a shorter 12-hour stay can pay ₹1,000

title: 800 workers quit Trinamool, join P.K. Sasi’s DMF in Palakkad
content: Efforts are also under way to attract those who have either severed ties with the CPI(M) or have had bitter experience from the party leadership in the district

title: Congress will succumb to Jamaat’s demands, says Kerala BJP leader S. Suresh
content: BJP State general secretary says the Congress' failure to choose a CM candidate even nine days after the United Democratic Front emerged victorious in the Assembly elections is an insult to all Malayalis

title: Composition of governing bodies in Calicut varsity to change
content: With the UDF coming to power, the strength in the university’s senate could rise to 41 members. The number of CPI(M) members may fall to 20

title: Kerala Assembly polls 2026: IUML growing restive over Congress delaying CM decision
content: IUML leadership meeting at Panakkad may witness strong emotions unless CM is chosen by Wednesday morning

title: NEET-UG 2026 cancelled LIVE: Process to hold re-exam to begin in 7-10 days: NTA DG Abhishek Singh
content: The cancellation of the NEET-UG 2026 examination, following an alleged question paper leak, has led to widespread political and student outrage across States

title: Corporations, BWSSB ramp up preparations ahead of monsoon
content: Measures include stormwater drain maintenance, road repairs, waste clearance, and identification of dangerous structures to prevent flooding and rain-related incidents across the city

title: Despite 50 per cent rainfall deficit, Kodagu braces for intense monsoon
content: Zilla Panchayat CEO Anand Prakash Meena says Kodagu is experiencing unusually dry conditions this year and warns that the district could face both drought-like conditions and episodes of excessive rainfall in the coming months.

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly: From Udhayanidhi Stalin finding common ground with Speaker Prabhakar to AMMK’s Kamaraj praising CM Vijay
content: Two AIADMK members extended greetings to Speaker, Udhayanidhi Stalin says Sanatanam that divides people should be abolished, among others in today’s Assembly proceedings

title: Intellectuals’ forum seeks clarity on river water sharing talks
content: Alochaanparula Vedika raises concern over proposals discussed by Chief Minister with Union Jal Shakti Minister; opposes Palamuru-Rangareddy project and upstream diversion of Godavari waters

title: Nandyal MP flags off daytime train service from Guntakal to Markapur road
content: Shabari says that efforts are underway to further modernise the Nandyal Railway Station to provide modern amenities, affordable fares, and safe travel experiences for rail passengers

title: Elephant carcass found in Nilambur
content: The elephant is suspected to have slipped from a cliff nearly 30 metres high before crashing onto the concrete structure below, say forest officials

title: Fire breaks out in Guntur government hospital lab; no casualties reported
content: GGH Superintendent suspects that the continuous use of air conditioners and other electronic equipment during the peak summer season might have caused an electrical short circuit, leading to the fire

title: Andhra Pradesh records 18% rise in rabi paddy procurement, says Civil Supplies Minister
content: Nadendla Manohar says 19.70 lakh metric tonnes procured so far against target of 27 lakh metric tonnes; ₹4,600 crore credited to 1.9 lakh farmers within 24 hours

title: Kanakagiri Utsav to feature cultural events, drone show, sports events
content: On Saturday, a procession featuring 40 folk and cultural troupes will be taken out from Eduru Hanumappa Temple to Kanakachalapathi Temple

title: Two cheetahs released into wild in Kuno; CM Yadav says M.P. now recognised as ‘Cheetah State’
content: The objective of Project Cheetah is to restore the endangered animal species in the state, increase their numbers, and prepare them for free hunting and roaming

title: Pemmasani seeks Centre’s intervention to resolve traffic woes at Guntur bypass
content: The Union Minister of State for Rural Development and Communications also flags a rise in road accidents in the area and stresses the need for immediate corrective measures in the interest of public safety

title: Returnable plots allotted to 98% of Amaravati land pooling farmers, says Narayana
content: According to official data, 63,295 of 70,824 returnable plots, accounting for 89%, had been registered while 7,529 plots remained pending

title: YSRCP alleges nexus behind aqua feed price hike
content: Former Ministers accuse coalition government of shielding feed companies, demand rollback of increased prices and warn of agitation over farmers’ hardships

title: Kadapa’s smart kitchen sheds to launch on June 1
content: District Collector instructs officials to complete the construction work by May 28, conduct two days of trial runs, and ensure that all necessary infrastructure is in place ahead of the reopening of schools

title: Stampede case: Karnataka High Court permits DNA executives to travel abroad
content: The executives were arraigned as accused in the criminal cases related to the stampede that occurred outside M. Chinnaswamy stadium on June 4, 2025

title: CITU stages protest against new Labour Codes
content: Members burn copies of the Labour Codes and raise slogans condemning the Centre’s decision to implement them after finalising rules this month

title: Development of new lotus varieties brings Ajitha national recognition
content: A couple of new lotus varieties developed by her -- Mayoori and Paneer Rose -- have now received registration from the Protection of Plant Varieties and Farmers’ Rights Authority of the Government of India

title: Tamil Nadu politics LIVE: AIADMK reacts sharply to rebel MLAs, AMMK expels party's lone legislator
content: Ahead of the crucial floor test where TVK-led government is set to prove its majority in the Legislative Assembly and amid a split in AIADMK, AMMK's lone MLA S. Kamaraj expelled from party for announcing his support to TVK.

title: Uncertainty prevails over university faculty recruitment drive in A.P. on May 15
content: The recruitment drive is caught in a legal tangle, with 20 writ pleas related to 2023 university recruitment notifications pending before A.P. High Court, says Assistant Professor APPSC Exam Qualified Candidates’ Association

title: CPI seeks Supreme Court-monitored probe into alleged NEET-2026 paper leak
content: CPI State secretary alleges reports were circulating about the involvement of major Andhra-based corporate groups in the leak racket

title: Tehran, Taiwan, Trade: Trump Heads To China With Xi Holding The Upper Hand
content: Despite years of harsh rhetoric on China, Donald Trump has continued to publicly praise Chinese President Xi Jinping. Just last week, he described Xi as a "good man" and a "smart man" with whom he has "a very good relationship".

title: Delhi Maximum Temperature Settles At 39 Degrees, Air Quality Remains Moderate
content: According to IMD data, Safdarjung, the city's primary weather station, recorded a maximum temperature of 39 degrees Celsius (0.5 degrees below normal), and no rainfall was recorded there until 5:30 pm.

title: Tamil Nadu Government Formation LIVE Updates: Vijay Meets Allies To Rally Support As Row Erupts Over Astrologer's Appointment
content: Vijay's oath ceremony, which took place on Sunday, came after four days of protracted political negotiations after his party received an unprecedented mandate that split open the binary politics that Tamil Nadu has been witnessing for over 50 years

title: Nuke, Line And Sinker: Last Voyage Of North Korea-Bound Russian Ship
content: The Ursa Major, also known as Sparta 3, left the Russian fuel port of Ust-Luga in the Gulf of Finland in early December 2024.

title: Day After PM Modi's Fuel Appeal, BJP Leader's 200 Car Convoy Chokes Bhopal
content: The convoy caused long jams across multiple stretches, with commuters, children, elderly citizens, and daily travelers stranded in extreme heat while the political spectacle rolled through.

title: Vijay Appoints Astrologer His Political Adviser, "Unacceptable," Say Allies
content: Pandit, known for his dual role as an astrologer and a spokesperson for the TVK, has been a close aide to the leadership during the recent electoral campaign.

title: Adani Ports Partners With Oceaneering To Expand Offshore Operations In Europe
content: The agreement is aimed at strengthening APSEZ's strategy to build one of the world's largest integrated marine platforms spanning logistics, offshore support, underwater engineering and deepwater infrastructure services.

title: Vijay Meets AIADMK 'Rebels' As EPS Rival Shanmugam Says "Ready To Back TVK"
content: AIADMK asserted that alliance decisions could not be taken by a handful of MLAs and maintained that party cadre remained firmly behind Edappaddi K Palaniswami.

title: Who Is Rickey Pandit, Astrologer Who Predicted TVK Win, Now Vijay's Adviser
content: He claims he predicted Jayalalithaa's victory in the 1991 elections and also warned her about a difficult phase between 1994 and 1997.

title: India's "Reputation, Standing" Reply To China's "On-Site" Support To Pak
content: In retaliation for the April 2025 Pahalgam attack that killed 26, New Delhi launched Operation Sindoor, dismantling terror infrastructure in Pakistan and PoK

title: 'Don't Twist Words': VCK Chief On Dalit Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Comment
content: VCK chief Thol Thirumavalavan denied media reports that said he was approached by both AIADMK and DMK to become chief minister if the two rival parties managed to form an alliance to stop the TVK from taking power

title: Maharashtra Opposition Demands All-Party Meeting Over PM's Austerity Appeal
content: The prime minister has appealed to citizens to avoid non-essential gold purchases, reduce petrol and diesel consumption, postpone foreign travel, and adopt practices such as working from home.

title: NEET-UG Paper Leak Turns Into New Battlefield For Opposition And Centre
content: The 'patient zero' of the NEET-UG paper leak for admissions to medical studies has been traced allegedly to Maharashtra's Nashik, people with direct knowledge of the matter said

title: "Why Weren't You Serious?" Top Court To Probe Agency In Bengal Coal Fraud Case
content: Senior advocate Siddhartha Dave, appearing for accused Anup Majee, said he had cooperated in the investigation by appearing before the agency 23 times.

title: Opinion: Opinion | NEET Paper Leak: When Incompetence Becomes The System
content: India had been here before, exactly two years ago. The language was the same. The apology was the same. The promise was the same. Only the year had changed.

title: CBI Raids Punjab Vigilance Office In Mohali, 3 Arrested In Bribery Case
content: Punjab BJP President Sunil Jakhar commented on the Vigilance Department's handling of cases involving common citizens, stating that the department has become a "cancer" for the poor people of Punjab.

title: On Camera, Woman Dragged On Road By Bike-Borne Mobile Snatchers
content: The incident took place in the New Kirti Nagar locality under the jurisdiction of Moti Nagar Police Station.

title: Petrol & diesel prices frozen for 4 years - how deep is India's fuel dilemma?
content: India assures stable fuel supplies despite global disruptions and rising crude prices. However, unchanged domestic rates are causing significant losses for oil marketing companies, leading to a pressing question about potential price hikes. The government faces a fiscal burden from absorbing price shocks through tax cuts.

title: 'Sent from my Windows Phone': Old email screenshot reveals Nadella-Altman bond since 2015
content: A recent X screenshot reveals Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella's early 2015 emails with OpenAI's Sam Altman, predating ChatGPT's fame. These exchanges, sent from a Windows Phone, highlight a decade-long strategic dialogue, not a sudden partnership. This resurfaces Nadella's regret over exiting the smartphone market, a decision he called a 'biggest mistake'.

title: Meta to cut 8,000 jobs and CEO Mark Zuckerberg is making priorities clear for all employees
content: Meta is cutting 8,000 jobs on May 20 as CEO Mark Zuckerberg rebuilds the company around small, AI-powered teams. With capex hitting a record $125–145 billion this year, CFO Susan Li admitted there's no clean answer for what Meta's ideal headcount even looks like anymore. The company wants builders who can ship alone what teams once built together—and is letting everyone else go.

title: Will Varun Chakravarthy play vs RCB? Massive update on KKR mystery spinner
content: Kolkata Knight Riders face a must-win IPL 2026 clash against Royal Challengers Bengaluru, with star spinner Varun Chakravarthy's fitness a major concern. Assistant coach Shane Watson expressed optimism about the team's recent form and potential playoff push, highlighting Angkrish Raghuvanshi's impressive skill development.

title: US govt agency releases 'Public Notice' in America using banned drones and routers
content: The FCC has extended the software update deadline for foreign-made drones and routers already in use across the US, pushing the cutoff from early 2027 to January 1, 2029. The waiver covers security patches, bug fixes, and compatibility updates for previously authorized devices on the agency's Covered List. New foreign-made models remain banned unless manufacturers secure conditional approval from the Department of Defense or Homeland Security.

title: Rs 16.77L cr gone! Market crash over 4 days leaves investors with deep losses; what's next?
content: Persistent foreign fund outflows and the rupee sliding to record lows have further dampened sentiment, prompting investors to stay cautious and intensify selling across equity markets.

title: R Madhavan shares parenting lesson he learnt while raising son Vedaant
content: R Madhavan emphasizes instilling gratitude and awareness of privilege in his son Vedaant, contrasting extravagant modern parenting with grounded values. He advocates for filling children's time with passionate activities until adolescence to prevent dwelling on negative influences, a lesson learned from a Canadian family.

title: NEET UG 2026 leak: Rahul asks students to ‘Google’ ex-NTA DG’s posting; attacks BJP
content: Congress leader Rahul Gandhi intensified his attack on the Centre after the cancellation of NEET-UG 2026, asking students to “Google” the current posting of the former NTA Director General linked to the NEET-UG 2024 controversy. In a strongly worded X post, Gandhi alleged that the BJP government protects and promotes officials associated with examination irregularities. His remarks came hours after the NTA cancelled NEET-UG 2026 and ordered a fresh examination amid allegations of paper leaks and widespread irregularities.

title: NHL rumor links Matthew Knies with Blackhawks, Canadiens, Utah Mammoth
content: Matthew Knies has become one of the hottest names in the latest NHL trade rumors after reports connected the Toronto Maple Leafs winger to a handful of interested teams. After another underwhelming season across the National Hockey League, Toronto is looking at possible roster changes in the offseason, with the Chicago Blackhawks, Montreal Canadiens and Utah Mammoth reportedly watching closely.

title: Dianna Russini and husband stay public amid Mike Vrabel controversy
content: Mike Vrabel, the New England Patriots’ head coach, and the former New York Times’ popular NFL insider, Dianna Russini, have remained distant from each other ever since their affair rumors sparked outrage among millions around the world. As Dianna Russini stays at home after resigning from her position at The Athletic, all eyes have been on her husband, Kevin Goldschmidt.

title: Ronda Rousey vs Gina Carano: Date, time and Netflix streaming details
content: MMA legend Ronda Rousey is finally set to make her long-awaited return to combat sports this Saturday in a blockbuster fight against Gina Carano. The highly anticipated clash will headline the first-ever live MMA event streamed on Netflix. Fans have waited years to see the two pioneers of women’s MMA face each other after talks of a fight first surfaced more than a decade ago.

title: 'Over 100 matching questions': Rajasthan 'guess paper' clue blows open NEET-UG 2026 scandal
content: The National Testing Agency has cancelled the NEET-UG 2026 examination. This decision follows evidence of a significant overlap between circulated guess papers and the actual question paper. The Central Bureau of Investigation will now conduct a comprehensive probe into the alleged paper leak and malpractice network. Over 24 lakh aspirants are affected by this unprecedented move.

title: Why Gujarat Titans changed jersey colour vs Sunrisers Hyderabad
content: Pat Cummins won the toss and chose to field as Sunrisers Hyderabad faced Gujarat Titans. The Titans sported a special lavender jersey to support cancer awareness. Both teams, with identical points, retained their playing XIs for the crucial IPL 2026 encounter. Captains Cummins and Gill expressed confidence in their strategies.

title: Israel sent Iron Dome system to UAE to defend against attacks by Iran
content: Israel has reportedly deployed its Iron Dome air defence system to protect the UAE, a significant development following recent Iranian drone and missile attacks on the Gulf nation. This cooperation, confirmed by US Ambassador Mike Huckabee, highlights deepening ties between the two Abraham Accord signatories. The UAE has faced extensive aerial assaults, prompting this strategic military assistance.

title: Why Vijay's floor test in Tamil Nadu is really a survival test for AIADMK
content: Tamil Nadu's trust vote for CM-designate Vijay is overshadowed by a potential AIADMK rupture. Senior rebels are questioning party chief Palaniswami's leadership, accusing him of seeking an understanding with the rival DMK. This internal strife could signal the AIADMK's terminal decline as its MLAs consider shifting loyalty.

title: Kaante dispute: Pritish Nandy Communications gets Rs 2.55 crore settlement from Bombay HC
content: The Bombay High Court has officially disposed of the commercial appeal regarding the 2002 film 'Kaante', following a settlement between Pritish Nandy Communications and White Feather Films. As per the consent terms approved on April 21, 2026, Sanjay Gupta’s production house will pay a lump sum of Rs. 2.55 crore to PNC within 60 days. This settlement resolves a 2016 arbitration dispute that originally involved a claim of Rs. 3.52 crore. Failure to pay within the stipulated window will result in the resumption of original litigation proceedings.

title: 822/4 and a 794-run win! Cricket match produces unreal scorecard
content: In a Zimbabwe domestic cricket match, Scorpions Cricket Club achieved an astonishing 822/4, powered by centuries from Winfed Mutende (203 off 75 balls) and Takunda Madembo (302). Methane Lions collapsed to 28/7, resulting in a record 794-run victory for Scorpions. This score dwarfs previous high totals in List A cricket.

title: ‘A mature captain never ... ': Ex-India cricketer questions Axar Patel’s leadership
content: Mohammad Kaif criticized Axar Patel for publicly singling out teammate Kuldeep Yadav during Delhi Capitals' challenging IPL 2026 season. Kaif emphasized that experienced captains avoid public criticism, especially of proven performers like Kuldeep, advocating for team-focused accountability instead.  Despite this, Axar played a crucial captain's knock in a recent win.

title: Tamil Nadu CM Vijay meets AIADMK rebel leaders ahead of crucial floor test
content: Tamil Nadu faces an AIADMK internal conflict as Vijay's new government prepares for a floor test. A faction led by CV Shanmugam pledged support to Vijay, defying party directives. Agri SS Krishnamurthy warned rebel MLAs of action, while OS Manian asserted 47 MLAs back Edappadi K Palaniswami. This rift highlights deep divisions within the AIADMK following recent poll performance.

title: Mouni Roy's husband Suraj Nambiar deletes his Instagram account amid divorce rumours
content: Speculation about Mouni Roy and Suraj Nambiar's marriage intensified as they reportedly unfollowed each other on Instagram, with Suraj's account now appearing deactivated. Adding to the buzz, Mouni's friend Disha Patani also allegedly unfollowed Suraj. Fans expressed concern over the alleged separation, recalling the couple's 2022 wedding.

title: The superpower trap: Who has more cards - Trump or Xi?
content: President Trump's China visit occurs amid a US-Iran stalemate, with analysts suggesting Xi Jinping holds more leverage. Many in Beijing view Trump as a symptom of liberal democracy's instability and American decline, validating Xi's "rise of the East" worldview. This perception of US weakness could lead China to miscalculate and test Washington.

title: India’s retail inflation jumps to over one-year high at 3.48 per cent in April
content: India's retail inflation climbed to a 13-month high of 3.48% in April, primarily fueled by soaring food prices. Consumer Food Price Index accelerated to 4.20%, with rural inflation outpacing urban rates. While silver and gold jewellery saw significant price hikes, tomatoes surged, but potatoes and onions experienced deflation. This marks the second consecutive monthly rise, nearing the RBI's target.

title: 'Those eyes don't lie': Krunal Pandya on Virat Kohli
content: Krunal Pandya continues to be a vital all-rounder for RCB in their IPL 2026 title defence, recently scoring a match-winning 73 despite severe cramps against Mumbai Indians. He also achieved his 100th IPL wicket and draws inspiration from Virat Kohli's unwavering intensity and dedication, having previously won Player of the Match in two IPL finals.

title: 'Mere liye raita phaila kar gayi hain': Brij Bhushan on Vinesh Phogat controversy
content: Former WFI president Brij Bhushan Sharan Singh stated that Vinesh Phogat's participation in wrestling trials is under the current WFI administration's purview. He defended his tenure, asserting the WFI never barred athletes without valid reasons. Phogat was declared ineligible by the WFI until 2026 due to ongoing disciplinary proceedings, a decision she disputes, citing international clearance.

title: NEET-UG 2026 cancelled: How a 'guess paper' leak derailed the exam and what happens next
content: NEET-UG 2026 exam has been cancelled by the National Testing Agency due to a massive "guess paper" leak, impacting over 22 lakh students. The government has ordered a CBI probe into the alleged irregularities. The re-examination dates will be announced soon, with existing registrations carrying forward. This decision aims to protect the integrity of the national examination system.

title: Dianna Russini’s public moment sparks “staged PR” claims online
content: Dianna Russini and her husband Kevin Goldschmidt sparked intense online speculation after photos of the couple kissing outside their New Jersey home went viral. The public display of affection came amid ongoing rumours linking Russini to Patriots coach Mike Vrabel, leading many social media users to question whether the moment was staged. Some even floated theories about an alleged open relationship, though no evidence supports those claims. The controversy surrounding Russini and Vrabel continues dominating online conversations.

title: Rs 10 lakh crore wiped out! Sensex tanks 1,400 points - top reasons for crash
content: One of the key triggers behind the market decline was renewed uncertainty surrounding the US-Iran conflict. US President Donald Trump said the ceasefire with Iran was “on life support” after Tehran rejected Washington’s latest proposal aimed at ending the conflict. Trump described Iran’s demands as “garbage.”

title: What next after PM Modi's appeal on gold, work from home - 10 things to know
content: PM Modi has called on citizens to adopt measures aimed at conserving foreign exchange, as the surge in oil prices triggered by the Iran conflict has intensified pressure on the rupee and strained India’s foreign exchange reserves.

title: Haryana Board declares Class 12 result 2026 with 84.67% pass rate as girls outperform boys
content: The Board of School Education Haryana has declared the Senior Secondary (Academic) Examination result for February/March 2026 at www.bseh.org.in. The overall pass percentage stands at 84.67%, with girls outperforming boys by 6.52%. Science recorded the highest stream-wise result at 90.08%. A total of 2,42,856 students appeared, and 2,05,618 passed. Charkhi Dadri topped district performance, while Nuh was lowest. Re-evaluation applications will remain open for 20 days after result declaration.

title: Who is Suraj Nambiar? Mouni Roy's husband as divorce rumours buzz
content: Mouni Roy and Suraj Nambiar are facing divorce rumours after fans noticed Mouni unfollowed him on social media. Suraj is a Bengaluru-born businessman and investment banker based in Dubai. The couple married in January 2022 in Goa and have launched two business ventures together including a restaurant called Badmaash.

title: Mike Vrabel’s resurfaced Dianna Russini remark fuels affair rumor buzz
content: Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini’s controversy intensified after an old Barstool Feud video resurfaced online amid ongoing affair rumours. In the clip, Russini answered “Kiss her” during a discussion about how “to get a woman in the mood,” prompting Vrabel to respond, “I’m paying very close attention… I’m so excited.” The viral exchange sparked fresh online speculation as fans continued revisiting past interactions, leaked photos and videos involving the Patriots coach and former NFL insider.

title: Oly medallist Sakshi speaks out on Vinesh debacle, seeks PM Modi’s help
content: Olympic medallist Sakshi Malik has backed Vinesh Phogat in her dispute with the Wrestling Federation of India over returning from retirement. Sakshi urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi and sports minister Mansukh Mandaviya to intervene, saying many countries support athletes after motherhood. Vinesh, now a Congress MLA and mother of a 10-month-old, is challenging WFI’s six-month comeback rule.

title: One vote, two Tirupatturs: DMK flags postal ballot mix-up after HC order
content: A single vote separated winners in a Tamil Nadu election, leading to a Madras High Court order.  The court barred a TVK MLA from voting in the assembly floor test due to a potential postal vote mix-up.  The DMK claims a recount could equalize votes, necessitating a coin toss.  The election officer cited EVM issues and postal ballot re-verification.
